mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trajectory in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing below 5.5% signals a labor market inflection point that triggers corporate hiring expansion and consumer spending growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops below 5.5% within a single month
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns average negative when UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the signal produces no statistically significant alpha over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and residential fixed investment amplify aggregate demand shocks, leading to sustained earnings revisions that lift S&P 500 prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint HOUST and RSAFS increases produce negative or flat 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of observations, invalidating the demand amplification premise.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaching the 105 index level during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates capacity utilization peaks that precede margin compression, yet short-term momentum drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns turn negative when INDPRO sustains values above 105 for two consecutive months, indicating the momentum premise fails under supply constraints.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index jump above 0.4% triggers market expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL monthly changes exceed 0.4%, or the market fails to price in tightening expectations within the 20-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with sub-6% unemployment creates a wage-price spiral risk that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy longer than priced in, fueling equity risk appetite over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k in 3 months AND UNRATE < 6%
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS and UNRATE conditions yield negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, disproving the accommodative policy persistence channel.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, invalidating the direct GDP-to-equity transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 900 thousand units during a monetary tightening cycle signals a demand shock that transmits to broader economic activity, depressing S&P 500 valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when HOUST drops below 900 during rate hike periods, indicating the housing demand shock fails to propagate to equity valuations.
